# PumpKit

> Open-source TypeScript framework for building PumpFun Telegram bots on Solana.

## Overview

PumpKit provides production-tested building blocks for PumpFun bot development: Grammy bot scaffolding, Solana event monitors, HTML message formatters, SQLite storage, REST APIs, webhooks, health checks, and deployment configs. Ship a bot in hours, not weeks.

## Packages

- @pumpkit/core — Shared framework: bot scaffolding, monitors, formatters, storage, config, health checks
- @pumpkit/monitor — All-in-one PumpFun monitor bot (fee claims, launches, graduations, whale trades, CTOs)
- @pumpkit/tracker — Group call-tracking bot with leaderboards, PNL cards, and rankings
- @pumpkit/channel — Read-only Telegram channel feed for token events
- @pumpkit/claim — Fee claim tracker by token CA or X handle

## Event Types

PumpKit monitors 6 on-chain PumpFun event types:
1. Fee claims — creator fee claim notifications
2. Token launches — new token mint detection
3. Graduations — bonding curve completion → AMM migration
4. Whale trades — large buy/sell above configurable thresholds
5. CTO alerts — Creator Takeover (fee redirection) detection
6. Fee distributions — shareholder payout notifications

## Tech Stack

TypeScript, Grammy (Telegram), @solana/web3.js, better-sqlite3, tsup, Turborepo, Docker, Railway.

## Links

- GitHub: https://github.com/pumpkit/pumpkit
- Documentation: https://pumpkit.dev
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/org/pumpkit
- Parent project: https://github.com/nirholas/pump-fun-sdk

## Quick Start

```bash
npm install @pumpkit/core
```

```typescript
import { createBot, ClaimMonitor, formatClaim, createHealthServer } from '@pumpkit/core';

const bot = createBot({ token: process.env.BOT_TOKEN! });
const monitor = new ClaimMonitor({
  rpcUrl: process.env.SOLANA_RPC_URL!,
  onClaim: async (event) => await bot.broadcast(formatClaim(event)),
});
createHealthServer({ port: 3000, monitor });
monitor.start();
bot.launch();
```
